Steve Vinter

← Recipes

Sesame Ginger Noodles

with Stir-Fried Greens & Toasted Peanuts

SERVINGS: 2(4) | COOK TIME: 25(35) MINS | SPICY 🌶 | LESS PREP ⚡

Remember to wash all produce.

Ingredients

  • 1 oz ginger, peeled and minced
  • 1 tbsp tamari
  • 2 tbsp peanut butter
  • 2 tbsp rice vinegar
  • 2 tbsp sesame oil (divided)
  • 1 tbsp chili garlic sauce (divided) 🌶
  • 10 oz high-fiber ramen noodles
  • ¼ cup peanuts
  • 6 oz gai lan, chopped into 1-inch pieces
  • 4 oz green Swiss chard, thinly sliced
  • 2 mini sweet peppers, thinly sliced

From your kitchen

  • Salt
  • Pepper

Tools

  • Large pot
  • Large nonstick skillet

Ingredients are listed for 2 servings. If you're making 4 servings, please double your ingredients by using both meal kit bags provided.

Directions

  1. Mix the sesame ginger sauce — Bring large pot of salted water to a boil for noodles. Add ginger, tamari, peanut butter, rice vinegar, just half the sesame oil, just half the chili garlic sauce, and ¼ cup hot water to medium bowl and whisk sesame ginger sauce. (4-serving meal: use ½ cup hot water) TIPS: Sauce will not be completely smooth. Use more chili garlic sauce if you prefer more spice.

  2. Cook the noodles and toast the peanuts — Add ramen noodles to boiling water and cook until al dente, 1 to 2 minutes. Drain noodles and rinse under cold water to stop cooking process. Heat remaining sesame oil in large nonstick skillet over medium heat. Add peanuts and a pinch of salt and pepper. Cook until nuts are toasted and fragrant, 1 to 3 minutes. Transfer toasted peanuts to small bowl with slotted spoon, leaving oil in skillet. TIP: When peanuts become fragrant, remove them from skillet. They will continue to toast slightly while off heat.

  3. Stir fry the greens — Return skillet with sesame oil to high heat. Add gai lan, Swiss chard, and a pinch of salt and pepper and cook until bright green and tender, 2 to 3 minutes. TIP: If you're making the 4-serving meal, cook everything in large pot instead of skillet.

  4. Toss the noodles — Add cooked ramen noodles and sesame ginger sauce to skillet with greens. Cook, tossing frequently, until evenly coated and warmed through, 1 to 3 minutes.

  5. Serve — Divide sesame ginger noodles with stir-fried greens between bowls. Top with toasted peanuts and mini sweet peppers. Drizzle with remaining chili garlic sauce. Bon appétit!

Nutrition

Nutrition per serving (as packaged) Calories: 710, Fat: 29 g, Sodium: 1150 mg, Carbohydrates: 90 g, Protein: 25 g.

Allergens: peanut, sesame, soy, wheat. Manufactured on equipment shared with tree nuts, peanut, sesame, wheat, soy, egg, milk, fish, and shellfish.

Recipe from Purple Carrot.
